betterquesting:10.desc:8:
§6Heating Oil§f

§aA versatile heating fuel and chemical feedstock — fraction it from naphtha or industrial oil for further refining!§f

Heating Oil is a medium-value fluid produced as a co-product when processing §bNaphtha§f or §bIndustrial Oil§f in the §bFractioning Tower§f. It has a dark brown appearance and serves as an intermediate feedstock for catalytic reforming and other advanced processes.

§6Production§f:
§b• Fractioning Tower§f (from Naphtha): §e100mB§f Naphtha → §e60mB§f Diesel + §e40mB§f Heating Oil.
§b• Fractioning Tower§f (from Industrial Oil): §e100mB§f Industrial Oil → §e60mB§f Heating Oil + §e40mB§f Engine Lubricant.

§6Uses§f:
§b• Catalytic Reformer§f: Process §e1,000mB§f Heating Oil → §e500mB§f Naphtha + §e150mB§f Petroleum Gas + §e100mB§f Liquid Hydrogen.
§b• Fractioning Tower§f (secondary): Process further into smaller fractions depending on cracking chain.

§eTip§f: Heating Oil's primary value is as a feedstock for the §bCatalytic Reformer§f, where it can be upgraded back into Naphtha and Petroleum Gas. Stockpile it for when you have access to reforming!
